name: yet-ai
description: Yet AI local runtime host for VS Code.

commands.title: Yet AI: Set Local Runtime Session Token
Yet AI: Clear Local Runtime Session Token

yetai.runtimeUrl.description: Local Yet AI runtime URL used by the webview shell. Use a loopback URL without userinfo, query, or fragment. auto/launch starts the local engine over http; connect may use an externally managed loopback https runtime.

yetai.sessionToken.description: Deprecated dev-preview fallback local runtime bearer/session token for debug connections. Use the Yet AI: Set Local Runtime Session Token command so the value is stored in VS Code SecretStorage. Do not store provider API keys here.

yetai.guiDevUrl.description: Optional loopback http or https GUI dev server URL without userinfo, query, or fragment. Leave empty to use packaged GUI assets or the built-in shell placeholder.

yetai.launchMode.description: Runtime startup mode. auto launches a discovered/configured local engine binary when available, connect only checks the configured loopback runtime, and launch requires a binary.

yetai.engineBinaryPath.description: Optional absolute path to the local yet-lsp engine binary. When set with auto or launch mode, the extension starts it with a per-session token.

yetai.lsp.enabled.description: Opt in to the read-only Yet AI LSP MVP. When enabled, the extension starts yet-lsp --lsp-stdio as a separate process from the HTTP chat runtime.

scripts.compile: tsc -p . && npm run check:identity && npm run check:webview-safety && npm run check:engine-connection
scripts.test: node out/controlledFileRead.test.js && node out/controlledEdit.test.js && node out/controlledCommandRun.test.js && node out/controlledLexicalSearch.test.js && node out/webview.test.js
scripts.prepare:preview: npm run copy:gui && npm run copy:identity && npm run compile
